textByHashes.4d0dee17eb87c8a367383acc4f858be7c2be06dd: Hello young one. Cross my palm with silver and the future will be revealed to you.
textByHashes.f4b56d5299220946aad91cada4eda1f704ab4c57: Come closer, and listen carefully to what the future holds for you, as I peer into the swirling mists of the crystal ball.
textByHashes.b6387dfedee43c4e04c6fcc5533a9492755cff88: I can see images forming. I can see you.
textByHashes.2d19d838e6ae248d6f303a3b68f9f601d4fb3415: You are holding a very impressive looking sword. I'm sure I recognise that sword...
textByHashes.f998aa350c5a7016576e90ca1e63972345272bee: There is a big dark shadow appearing now.
textByHashes.1f39668704a3be112d1a606b0bba2e3672a31d17: Oh! I really hope he didn't see me looking at him through my crystal ball!
textByHashes.9d71c4cd6008419e40f649dee28a62c668b28b48: He tried to destroy this city 150 years ago. He was stopped just in time by the great hero Wally.
textByHashes.7e5a3539c43a24adbc657e4f86f1c3773ef7f500: Using his magic sword Silverlight, Wally managed to trap the demon in the stone circle just south of this city.
textByHashes.2ba17e37b8d579876e73b7692235df8b08b1fdd7: Ye gods! Silverlight was the sword you were holding in my vision! You are the one destined to stop the demon this time.
textByHashes.d57e398030978dff0adc387a95fa7a43025d2a1c: If you face Delrith while he is still weak from being summoned, and use the correct weapon, you will not find the task too arduous.
textByHashes.5edabdf90a26088781df24fa6d0b6ba25f455184: Do not fear. If you follow the path of the great hero Wally, then you are sure to defeat the demon.
textByHashes.fecc8abd88aa99017f115233868c937c7babc737: Ah, the overconfidence of the young!
textByHashes.29a1adf0121fc40dd70dad36af3b125682a3909c: Delrith can't be harmed by ordinary weapons. You must face him using the same weapon that Wally used.
textByHashes.0084eec0a36fb12e943d2ee6d65d745ccacfb9a7: Yes I know. Maybe that is why history doesn't remember him. However he was a very great hero.
textByHashes.1bd27a797d357c1e05c6a0aec934e8a0f5db80b6: Who knows how much pain and suffering Delrith would have brought forth without Wally to stop him!
textByHashes.462ccb7e790638b8ea7073e482d0712e3a088a66: It looks like you are going to need to perform similar heroics.
textByHashes.f116536db9cee3fbf9512da948905ceb3060a9a8: Wally managed to arrive at the stone circle just as Delrith was summoned by a cult of chaos druids...
textByHashes.694f81cea0b397cbe1657f70f5e3ccec90c54d2e: By reciting the correct magical incantation, and thrusting Silverlight into Delrith while he was newly summoned, Wally was able to imprison Delrith in the stone block in the centre of the circle.
textByHashes.b38fdaaa5da11978a451c12fb600dfee9cbb7409: Delrith will come forth from the stone circle again.
textByHashes.997c5637c7707baf26fc0730e020b5b0960dfac9: I would imagine an evil sorcerer is already starting on the rituals to summon Delrith as we speak.
textByHashes.b048b2b92f95676bf91410b5e79cbbb4adea602e: Oh yes, let me think a second...
textByHashes.a585ce7be0787d509243cb6d4caf024988c7f4ae: Alright, I think I've got it now, it goes.... ... ... ... ... . Have you got that?
textByHashes.861e7ab41987b16ff4361f64b2afcb9980c85ce6: Silverlight has been passed down through Wally's descendants. I believe it is currently in the care of one of the King's knights called Sir Prysin.
textByHashes.094c0f2f1b9a640403dc89d78a2379bcdadba53c: He shouldn't be too hard to find. He lives in the royal palace in this city. Tell him Aris sent you.
textByHashes.046104ac5dcf507413323b90be7f36cf70ef30c6: Good luck, and may Guthix be with you!
textByHashes.ae5f061987c887adbaf1ee96e57248b55ba998ca: Well if you need any advice I'm always here, young one.
textByHashes.5b42579cc441c0b8ac63d63830d1879488d33f1a: In the scheme of things you are very young.
textByHashes.ae75f09c90cdd7dfe039f9857643fc0a66aab69c: Count the number of legs on the stools in the Blue Moon inn, and multiply that number by seven.
textByHashes.dd80ef7e8e145a3e544660f6c6b5ffef9e815614: You show wisdom for one so young.
textByHashes.f03b6216b635d2fda173f6e810fc6c437a2a3baf: Ok, hurry, we haven't much time.
textByHashes.391432470cdbc4cde50a489e831b7b98fec921ed: Just head south and you should find the stone circle just outside the city gate.
textByHashes.82083f75d9252ec51cf494ad0994f1cd3a7ed1f9: , you must help me defeat this evil!
textByHashes.6a3d448b4f4e2e86df99076b93f3276e550dc82e: Hello there adventurer. How can I assist you?
textByHashes.1d296b0ef998f2ca2e408d52011a581328c36592: I am the fortune-teller. My friends call me Aris.
textByHashes.c6f9f2d45a3f5d7449437a9d68438c57d3361900: Was there something you wanted to ask me about?
textByHashes.88db8a78d11c5779d33a7b141f4085606269ec0a: No, no I'm quite sure that I never have.
textByHashes.4f8f832fa68b95fff464c1a2c2586d1841f32d36: Certainly. That fellow over there in the chef's hat is called the Culinaromancer.
textByHashes.cbaba6f1753944d7d8798b4d3a2b7cd2f60d144f: A hundred years ago, he threatened the secret council with death, but was foiled by the quick thinking of his assistant, who managed to trap him in another dimension.
textByHashes.b2ef72a52e4adc0dc607f5ac87630d1956ac4404: As a result of that action, the assistant was offered a job for life as head cook of Lumbridge, for himself and all his future descendants.
textByHashes.62db4ff92f6c0bb335f47cb39c7fa86671740668: Now, apparently the current head cook of Lumbridge decided to recreate his ancestors greatest achievement, not realising that his achievement was not a meal but a powerful food-magic, and by recreating the spell he has
textByHashes.f24f918ea19c243670f97683cc7e03ad7584af22: inadvertently freed the evil Culinaromancer from his prison, to wreak havoc upon this world once more.
textByHashes.4519f2f4c66dcb8bf31bcc8ed0432bf7bbf0cd7b: Naturally, when I saw what was happening, I intervened with a simple Tempus Cessit spell, so that we would have time to immunise the secret council from his attack, and so that we can defeat the Culinaromancer
textByHashes.0f1ff392a2f68e9ed1253eb2ab51c66730c357a9: You want me to explain it again?
textByHashes.9508da1440b957676dae5bbd8c9fafdd4137f337: (sigh) Okay then: 100 years ago, the evil food magician was defeated and banished.
textByHashes.897a2c457a9e85268f48f49d5eba4e7c20c50dfc: Because of you and the Lumbridge cook, he is now free.
textByHashes.9e97f6c4315e28c558e7e2346921df59b21c228c: He was about to slaughter all of these people, but I stopped time so that you could protect them.
textByHashes.1261d701270cdb98117317c6a4dedbf9ae8d7caf: Yes I stopped time. And YOU have to do it partly because you helped release him by getting those ingredients, and partly because I am stuck here keeping the spell intact.
textByHashes.287ff64918b5d52d58593189ccde0a2a4f9437c5: Oh, unbelievably so. This is why I have limited the time bubble to this room and this room alone.
textByHashes.7c75e2e9c3aeb1890769353667e60b2dfa108426: Everything outside this room continues to flow through time. This is why outside of this room you have already defeated the Culinaromancer, and protected the
textByHashes.9ab52aa68aae7e40245b7de9520a8d7975b6b3ab: Inside this room however you have not.
textByHashes.5b80cafbbe88cee084f9fcc833b0c422a26d0287: Where time has been frozen in this room, outside it continues to flow, so this room is now currently in what we would call 'the past', meaning events inside here have already happened, and the longer time continues to flow
textByHashes.7364ef03d18c57fcd1bc4d3be8063d3e48697761: outside, the more removed from normal time this room becomes.
textByHashes.c1f08a8f8e9e1ffc8f8d52d6e8261fec20961bdc: Yes. But only outside. What you need to do now is save them in here as well, so that time can resume its normal ebb and flow.
textByHashes.6201a967e756128510dbb031d4f122211521cebc: Otherwise there will be increasing temporal pressure, and the entire universe might explode!
textByHashes.7822e335117e61f1f1109c346156951627c1afde: Yes. That is why it is so important that you protect them all.
textByHashes.a60e19d4ba11101e277eca5630b4fad85b68d13a: Yes. You have. In the future, which is also the present, but not in the present, which is also the past.
textByHashes.45591a603f0bf26eccae62e4c5fde36476922a8a: Yes, time travel tends to do that to people.
textByHashes.e57530509065ddda41e8f322ff6fc968795eb8ed: I suggest you don't worry about it too much, just make sure you protect each council member and then defeat the Culinaromancer.
textByHashes.52aa4066a0ff0ba57c6a6d630962ff5aba828bfd: Each council member will have their own unique dish that will serve to counteract the Culinaromancer's spell.
textByHashes.da7a042820b38b66ec6e910536dedb2ff2ef296f: Inspect the one you wish to save, and I will offer any advice I have.
textByHashes.f0c6759d147ac2df61af3b0e1ef19396712c5ad6: Was there anything else you wanted to ask me about?
textByHashes.d9d828f671790af44d7e1862d1ca816ab2311bdd: He calls himself the Culinaromancer. He is an extremely evil man, with incredibly powerful magics.
textByHashes.92f7fa2335936cc08e1b070c53e1a83841a5655e: Yes, that's correct. Just as an Oneiromancer draws their magical powers from dreams, or a necromancer draws their strength from the energies of the dead, a Culinaromancer uses
textByHashes.5b7ab4991d706c3000428d002f9774999910c107: food as his source of magical power.
textByHashes.ced4fbb6733ef2dea41d44f29724af366fc19639: Do not underestimate the latent power of foodstuffs as a source of magical energy.
textByHashes.f820ba9ac2670b8570be9d40662ce2a841bddfda: Not only are they a fundamental life-force, but they often evoke great emotional attachment.
textByHashes.7c41fd4f4fcfcbd495a862eed100c4c3397caa5e: Do not underestimate him, part of his strength lies in the fact his unique type of magical power is quite obscure, I have seen many fall before him.
textByHashes.7cfd2f2451c421f4bf1411632cb6e349fda84f4d: Ah, it is a very old story indeed.
textByHashes.e063e25f0581830713be342a5c67a0ca00df8df3: A hundred years ago, he worked here in Lumbridge castle for the then Duke as a cook.
textByHashes.1294cb534bd2d62c58769cb1bbf9a055a2830224: This was all a pretence, of course. As part of his duties he was allowed access to many exotic and esoteric foodstuffs. These allowed him to experiment with his magic and increase his power exponentially.
textByHashes.5f2d1c08fe9729b7918e50b21a4c78e1c571efc0: Building his power was just the first step however.
textByHashes.0aa3ee9fcff5dc826169062bcb72617400889529: Knowing of the regular meetings of the secret council here in Lumbridge, the Culinaromancer decided that he would eliminate all of them as the first step to his plans for world domination.
textByHashes.4f255405a91d9a7e066ffc673fa40a0f70e0dafe: Possibly, we might never know. As his plans were coming to fruition his assistant discovered them, and used his own magic against him, to trap him in another dimension where his powers would
textByHashes.fa8b9358c5ed09e33f2ab0d1f764c7d720bf6f0c: Well, the current cook of Lumbridge obviously discovered an ancient manuscript of his ancestors, and believing it to be a recipe decided to recreate the spell that banished him.
textByHashes.5ea46e721f4ec1b795a531a1ec67aa96bf90d19a: Which obviously seems to have opened the portal enough for him to escape back to this dimension. So this is partly your fault.
textByHashes.8230a8416ff5245809f4afce6f78d9fccd492535: Yes, those ingredients. So I hold you responsible for assisting me in ridding him of this dimension once and for all.
textByHashes.85c8bd0dfe746f8c2237a6ccffe7d3fa43353b26: Are you clear on what you have to do, or was there something you wished me to explain?
textByHashes.f99752740a7c3e0b29b9706a4981af2bc2184e89: The people you see assembled here are the self-styled secret council of Gielinor.
textByHashes.84f2c8fc391e1d1eb11aeda4fa1390fd6414d9dc: Many centuries ago, a small group from around the world of Gielinor decided that these lands needed to be directed, that it was too risky to leave the fates of this land to luck and the gods.
textByHashes.495fab525ae6ce7df4c88a39f1f6fa2d51f253b9: They decided that every 10 years they would meet to discuss current events, and shape how the world might then develop.
textByHashes.f046f8a2db1ccb9a08f741ef68bd00ed37b01d4a: No, not really. For all of their grand schemes and ideas, nothing ever gets done except every ten years they have a bit of a posh meal together and a bit of a gossip.
textByHashes.0b6e5fba9665d4cb030da1ce0af667025d883c1a: I don't really know what he thinks.
textByHashes.ae02a001348151403f76a3ff88638a3a38d3559e: Either way I'm not prepared to let him get away with a brazen attack on anybody while I have the power to stop him.
textByHashes.a2a6adc6df24420558e8baafaaf7fb0e3ddcad5d: And given that this is partly your fault, I fully expect your assistance in saving them.
textByHashes.3203d4ec4dd3b49dfe884e2b0ca0ad82f59db57a: Yes. You were the one who provided the cook with the ingredients that he needed to allow the Culinaromancer to return to this dimension.
textByHashes.92e6443ac61a4d063c548796000cc07c0a75d66f: What exactly is it that you don't understand?
textByHashes.f27937539d7646a947f743f0ca2a5b12e90149b0: Ah, that would be the time dilation effect.
textByHashes.1628d995ed4b59d75db4d17672dfcbbf14396b72: Yes. As time has stopped flowing in the normal way, your eyes are playing tricks on you, as the pictures absorbed by your eyes take slightly longer to reach your brain
textByHashes.423d26bbdd7981d3610194ecf9d90929336c3ef7: As everybody knows, time flows from north to south, so my freezing time means that this room has apparently stretched slightly along its horizontal axis.
textByHashes.7f380f952d7e8dd035abbc35042b1470346eb00d: Don't let it worry you too much. It's just an optical illusion, the room is exactly the same size as it always has been, it just appears to be a tiny bit bigger.
textByHashes.2b83947bf5a5ab4efaf61401201b87ffc1b5005d: Anything else you wanted to know?
textByHashes.c9810863811c4f9b0fed4efef962d98bbc65456a: Yes, I'm sure you have far more important things to do than SAVING THE ENTIRE UNIVERSE!
textByHashes.7256f031a77a6c32c9c5e7300600553e3a552e19: Don't talk to me! It might cause a time paradox!
textByHashes.e581a4672574085db9f353f437be6aa3b4fc8b97: LA-LA-LA I'M NOT TALKING TO YOU LA-LA-LA TIME PARADOX LA-LA-LA
textByHashes.2afe9fad729672a820ab0d6f1397ddbaebc369e6: Ask me how to protect him first, otherwise you will cause a time paradox! I'm keeping an eye on you, you know!
textByHashes.a205b7486ef0e9eeb67ab1ca36073b8ed375e5eb: Ask me how to protect them first, otherwise you will cause a time paradox! I'm keeping an eye on you, you know!
textByHashes.258507fd9bdd3f15e22828579f6cf7569707d37a: Now you've freed them all, I'll restart time so you can deal with the culinaromancer.
textByHashes.29f6b9e8596ba7c78c993d89c1a4e1288af1fe4f: We have broken your feeble spell! Begone!
textByHashes.f23e05e5f207fa59a25de376de179413ab148df2: One day he'll return. You must go through the portal and finish him off.
textByHashes.1490274fb22e840ac5ee85c9292582e0ca12bb47: Adventurer, you must hurry! You have freed the members of the secret council, but we must ensure that the Culinaromancer is defeated once and for all!
textByHashes.c6985bb331f309a4ec4f92f959b195864e24a6c6: Follow him into the portal and make sure he never leaves again!
textByHashes.a44aedfc090a28686c1a9679ad9a9ae98af81dc2: , I must warn you, inside that dimension you will have no assistance from the gods, and your prayers will not work at all!
textByHashes.3d7290be98fd6d37a4427c7962376790b7c4100e: Also be aware that he will use his powers to draw upon the might of foes you have fought before, and that dimensional instability means that your lost items will be irretrievable should you die...
textByHashes.dd7796fe513c402b8d7f89364e81dfc0d9549c2d: Take care, I suspect the Culinaromancer will not give up so easily!
textByHashes.d645cbc7bf5c0202c3b57a0341a4c91f09842a3b: Good work ! I think that will be the last we'll see of him! You'd better come back now.
textByHashes.eb4ea07cb445b25cf40cfb301f73b33ed9212e94: The council is meant to include one dark wizard, but obviously the other members did not want to include anyone who was a real threat.
textByHashes.fad5b794d46f2919a1e1f743005dc3c9a322e7de: To free Evil Dave you must make a stew, but it must be seasoned in a very particular way. The only way to find the recipe would be to ask someone who has tasted the stew.
textByHashes.3c2dc2fd6d55e65dae54d0459c1ccb2515c00eb4: Remember that from the point of view of the outside world, anything you do in here has already happened. All the people here are out in the world, because from their point of view you have already rescued them.
textByHashes.2919d606b0530bfe18d41369e82b68f4ad3365a5: In her basement. I make no judgement about that.
textByHashes.4ba77ce33d560bfd08b8c795a898e74f8967d467: Remember, you need to make a particular stew for Evil Dave. Speak to him outside the time warp, because from his point of view out there you have already rescued him so he has already eaten the stew. He lives in
textByHashes.19ff8dd50a62c1ce2af0b623cc09dcb81fc4f6d9: You should make sure that's the correct recipe first.
textByHashes.29dc6a42ba0d8231893742cc864bcbba0112848a: Good work adventurer, I've teleported them away to safety! You only have council member left to protect!
textByHashes.959fccd55a2e91949a147678f91416d7a857b49b: You will need to feed King Awowogei his favourite dish.
textByHashes.eca8ed4125e21a020f70d3f7f046b7251fc4bd06: I have to admit I'm not sure, maybe you should ask him?
textByHashes.c9788a6e4f3c7d88d4a0df1dcc001d441a69fe3d: Hmmm... Wait a moment, my fortune-teller-sense is tingling...
textByHashes.061ebb77951612703b0fc4c2b60355e17bee119b: Ah yes. To immunise him from the culinaromancer's attack, you will need to feed him fishcakes.
textByHashes.88e42ee1490b7f86874548c371413776b445d868: No... Like small bread crumbed fishy delicacies.
textByHashes.1f33b08d21f6d785a265edd4e796a200770dd6e3: I don't know. I am a fortune-teller, not a cook.
textByHashes.f2e3edde3eaf364f76dbcd524d16c9f953a1180c: I don't suppose it occurred to you to ask the actual cook in the room right next door to us?
textByHashes.44431789393196f51fc842ee35a4cdc37896fe90: Err... have you forgotten about the fishcakes already?
textByHashes.1ed2d2e2ff8a6b5854161e77db68e95d0d100cde: To immunise him from the culinaromancer's attack, you will need to feed him fishcakes.
textByHashes.bf1a38af50f60cad84a9b5b2a09884528f6982d8: And try not to forget this time!
textByHashes.e194a617c110d4c77494bf803d29509e8d06ae4f: Stop messing about and use the fishcakes on him!
textByHashes.8d191acbe99431b2f5378aa3ee6be5d371648bb2: Good work adventurer, I've teleported them away to safety! You only have council members left to protect!
textByHashes.7d17ccce1d2b03aa0451822db204a6a5b64b6c22: To protect this noble knight from the magic of the culinaromancer, you will have to give him a creme brulee.
textByHashes.0f8eb97cb9e6fea4f5a7bf32fd81b41bd4381049: Not just any creme brulee mind you, but a creme brulee supreme!
textByHashes.c44272b53ec4423c4198fedae3af510c7e962304: Are you going to constantly ask me questions?
textByHashes.3b7949fd54a0713a13e849ee9e2220d3985355ea: I am a fortune-teller, not a cook, and again I would suggest you speak to the cook next door who will be much more helpful on such culinary matters.
textByHashes.c1c51a93c41079d07af3819e79c8ca4ca262244e: Although I happen to know that one of the ingredients is the egg of the evil chicken.
textByHashes.0db4a4a5a057f5b8de2fd3a3c787e5e3543a287d: I believe the wise old man in Draynor has studied the Evil Chicken.
textByHashes.1507abb9d9ccef7e8814456dee7c920b3c2a883e: I would suggest you speak to him to get you on the right track. And tell him Bubbykins says hi.
textByHashes.8d71ac24b8c3b9afe51dc76f335349b467ee3964: That is not important right now. Go! Protect Sir Amik from the magic that threatens his life, my spell will only hold so long!
textByHashes.79a866e6a2d24a0f6219cd731905446a0fda5f3b: Skrach! He'd be un-frozen in a jiffy if he just had some Jubbly.
textByHashes.040d3a00a91bcaab9f56e55d2fab949bbce1191c: Sorry...Skrach, being an ogre likes Chompies, and a real delicacy is Jubbly Chompy bird. Do you think you could get some?
textByHashes.ce2398521c68b09127b25459811b0e49f8959327: Ah, well, that's where it breaks down a bit you see. The only 'thing' alive which might help you get the Jubbly Chompy is the most confusing beast that Gielinor has ever known.
textByHashes.87909cb42646a55ddd1b5c14b48b78e77933745a: The situation is lost I am afraid and I understand totally if you decide not to help Skrach.
textByHashes.f8027251be4c63543e4fd1c5b7b6c7b73fcf0010: I'm afraid it is. You see...you need to go and speak to an Ogre called Rantz over in the Feldip hills.
textByHashes.8da41f9e4346d671ee7053a8e89514defa33c7e2: How do you feel about getting Jubbly Chompy now and freeing Skrach?
textByHashes.9d4dd6a66045e9cda5bdfc909f7a55ab20de95b8: There's no excuse for him really...
textByHashes.11781fde2173de6c4e716816c6246144bd03f096: Well, I do pity you, and wish you the best of luck!
textByHashes.65162360e98422d7fbaeaa3b0f5602523821063f: Alas, I can certainly relate to what you say. However, he deserves to be set free, the same as everyone else.
textByHashes.2c6471c841a1a46599aae172ebc2b676654b74dc: Hmmm... neither of them were at the previous council meeting.
textByHashes.5cffa5b4bb7c6166f16fe843174739732eca25f2: The goblins get through leaders so fast I find it hard to keep track. I heard the last leader died during a game of pin the tail on the basilisk.
textByHashes.5e57a8cabf602b61543816cb78badfea32443d5c: Well, no worries. I'm sure if you just find something that they'll both eat then that should work. I hope.
textByHashes.abbc97c0e28b1c6a142ca1674ddb0650d2c92162: Well you could try asking some of the other goblins in their village for ideas. You should be able to find it just to the North of Falador, unless they've decided the sky is the wrong colour there or something.
textByHashes.a1e4c14c7c3e63cdaea0d8aa60003fc797460fb0: Well, what better to help the Lumbridge Guide than a 'Cake of Guidance'. Would you like to help?
textByHashes.81ece30eac8714348edfb2d34e0160d84350e43a: Well, I'm not too familiar with the fine details, you'll have to go and speak with Traiborn in the Wizards' Tower. He'll know what you need.
textByHashes.b325cd10bb3f10743c084a69012eb25a2f7f1858: Go have a word with Traiborn in the Wizards' Tower, he should be able to help you out with a Cake of Guidance!
textByHashes.fb384c4008f027031120d2c9b9f6641f4a16e316: What do you want? A prize? Use it on the Lumbridge Guide already!
textByHashes.eac9e6c901cd41fe50acf6c73c040261d6215da9: Best you go see Traiborn again then and see if he can help you.
textByHashes.3104e6e60189cda0d3c29e877a293e2c49c07917: To protect our short angry friend from the culinaromancer's magics, I suggest that you seek out his father within the mountain tunnel that he guards.
textByHashes.f7e2b3f714f4ec80ebbaa7a2b12887e9b5445c5d: His fondness for rock cakes could prove key in freeing him.
textByHashes.4e8f9ce686243b4f47cdde47076eb966ae5d7acf: STOP! What do you think you're doing!
textByHashes.179db115b1359a842f66bff33e70541f120eba69: You can't feed him red hot rock cakes! You'll kill him! Find a way to cool them down.
textByHashes.537b98d31ff43629ff7f93c51ac600440b5590d1: I hear there's a rather chilly reception on top of a rather icey mountain.
textByHashes.f271751114b44e4786b98e798ae55c6dd427e046: You're a hero now. That was a good bit of demonslaying.
textByHashes.185ceb528fce5dadd14165b9bdb24f7e54d60243: You forget. I'm good at knowing things.
textByHashes.a01372ba0ccbcc0a798d2d48440fa9d899dd13fa: I wouldn't worry about it too much if I were you. The world is safe once more thanks to your assistance.
textByHashes.5011b656bd0132c132d4f092b7beef920bcbd97e: Because I can tell the future.
textByHashes.357e581363eefe33ab893cb093bf9addebff2dc2: That is the answer to your next question.
textByHashes.7cfdbf5bf0aa7aa2e7e8674d533a9680c2c82e00: Thanks. Oh and be careful in the Wilderness. Tonight is not your night.
